ingredients: 1 packet fish head curry complete sauce (e.g., Prima Taste Fish Head Curry Complete Sauce)
700-800 g fish head or mixed fish cuts (e.g., red snapper, sea bream, grouper)
Spring onion and Chinese celery, roughly chopped
Optional: tamarind water or lime juice (for extra sourness)
Optional: coconut cream or evaporated milk (for a creamier gravy)
steps: Bring curry gravy to a boil: mix curry sauce packet with water in a pot. Add vegetables, herbs, and chilies and bring to a high boil.
Blanch fish head briefly in boiling water (about 10 seconds) to remove impurities; drain.
Add fish to the boiling curry gravy. Cover and simmer on low boil for about 6 minutes.
Flip fish pieces carefully, cover again and simmer another 6 minutes until cooked through.
Turn off heat and let the curry rest in the pot a few minutes to deepen flavor; reheat before serving if desired.
tips: Use a firm fish head and keep the simmer gentle so the fish doesn't break apart; add coconut cream at the end if you want a richer curry.
